title: SAC Jets Kill Two Monks at Seik Hkun Monastery, Sagaing — Thingyan Eve Airstrikes
detail: On April 13 — the eve of Myanmar's Thingyan Water Festival — SAC jet aircraft bombed Seik Hkun village west of Shwebo Township in Sagaing Region, striking a monastery and nearby school. Two monks were killed (ages 29 and approximately 50), eight novice monks were injured, and a pregnant woman sustained a leg wound; at least three other civilians were injured. Five bombs were dropped on the monastery, destroying dormitories, dining halls, and wells; nearby school buildings and homes were also damaged. Separate SAC airstrikes in the Tan Zin and Ponnagyun village areas of Sagaing Region were reported the same day. The timing of the strikes during the holiest period of Myanmar's Buddhist calendar — the four-day Thingyan festival when millions gather with family — drew widespread condemnation. The pattern of striking religious institutions mirrors the SAC's documented campaign against civilian infrastructure including churches, mosques, and Buddhist temples across conflict-affected states and regions.

media.caption: SAC jets bomb monastery and school in Sagaing Region on Thingyan eve, killing two monks

title: SAC Jets Drop Five Bombs on Hpakant on Thingyan Eve — KIA Drone Strikes Continue Through Day
detail: Fighting continued in Hpakant Township, Kachin State on April 13, extending from the escalation that began April 11. Two SAC fighter jets dropped approximately five bombs on the Ma Shi Ka Htaung neighborhood near Sutaunggon Mountain at around 1:00 PM local time; a separate drone attack was carried out at approximately 3:00 PM; a further drone strike followed later in the afternoon. Around 4:00 PM, approximately 100 SAC soldiers were observed deploying into the area. Most residents had previously evacuated, limiting immediate reported civilian casualties. The previous evening (April 12), clashes between KIA forces and the SAC military continued with KIA drone attacks. The sustained April 11–13 fighting represents the third major escalation in Hpakant since KIA captured six SAC frontline positions near Maw Wan Gyi on April 2. The jade-mining township generates critical revenue for the junta — an estimated $2–3 billion annually — making the KIA offensive economically as well as militarily significant.

media.caption: SAC military airstrikes continue in Hpakant Township during Thingyan; KIA drone operations persist

title: Junta Forces Burn ~200 Homes in North Lukan Village, Pakokku Township (Magway) on Thingyan Eve
detail: On April 13, Myanmar military forces burned approximately 200 homes to the ground in North Lukan village in Pakokku Township, Magway Region — located approximately 5 miles from Pakokku airfield. Residents had fled beforehand, aware of the military presence and prior incidents in surrounding townships. The scale of destruction — nearly the entire village — is consistent with the SAC's systematic village-burning campaign documented since 2021 across Sagaing and Magway regions. Pakokku Township, situated in the Irrawaddy heartland, has seen escalating resistance activity as PDF forces expand operations from Sagaing into Magway. The burning of North Lukan represents part of a deliberate counter-insurgency strategy of collective punishment targeting civilian populations in areas where resistance has support. Over 800 villages have been burned by SAC forces across Myanmar since the 2021 coup; Magway villages are increasingly targeted as the front line moves south.

media.caption: Civilian deaths continue from airstrikes and village burning as conflict deepens across Myanmar

title: SAC Gyrocopter Attacks Kill Infant in Salingyi and 14-Year-Old Girl in Yinmabin During Thingyan
detail: On April 13, the Myanmar military's Northwestern Regional Military Command deployed gyrocopter aircraft to attack villages in Sagaing Region during the Thingyan Water Festival. In Weikwe village, Salingyi Township, a gyrocopter opened fire on civilians, killing one infant struck by gunfire. In Leingnaut village in southern Yinmabin Township, a separate gyrocopter attack killed a 14-year-old girl. The use of gyrocopter (autogyro) aircraft for low-altitude attacks on villages has become a documented SAC tactic in Sagaing Region, where the relatively slow, low-flying aircraft allows for targeting of individuals in civilian settings with gunfire. The attacks on the eve and beginning of Thingyan — Myanmar's most sacred national holiday — mark a deliberate escalation in timing and add to the pattern of SAC strikes on civilians during Buddhist religious observances. The two deaths on April 13 in Sagaing bring to dozens the number of civilians killed by junta forces in the region during just the Thingyan period (April 13–16).

media.caption: Military gyrocopter attacks kill civilians including a toddler and teenager in Sagaing during Thingyan
